title: Wireglass Loadtest
description: JMeter metrics from loadtest/ WireglassLoadtestScenario, written by influxDbListener into the 'jmeter' bucket. Panels split by sampler label (transaction tag).

list.name: transaction
list.label: Sampler
list.query:
import "influxdata/influxdb/schema"
schema.tagValues(bucket: "jmeter", tag: "transaction")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value != "all" and r._value != "internal")

panels.title: Requests
panels.description: Total samples in the selected range. Read off the transaction=all rollup row that influxDbListener writes alongside the per-sampler rows.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "count")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction == "all" and r.statut == "all")
  |> sum()
  |> keep(columns: ["_value"])

panels.title: Errors
panels.description: Total failed samples. Green at zero is the expected state for a healthy demo run.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "countError")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction == "all" and r.statut == "all")
  |> sum()
  |> keep(columns: ["_value"])

panels.title: p95 (worst interval)
panels.description: Highest p95 any reporting interval saw. The slow tier's ~2s response dominates this number.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "pct95.0")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction == "all" and r.statut == "all")
  |> max()
  |> keep(columns: ["_value"])

panels.title: Active threads
panels.description: From the transaction=internal row JMeter emits for thread counts. Should sit at 3 during a run: one thread per tier.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r.transaction == "internal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "maxAT")
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

panels.title: Throughput by sampler
panels.description: Samples per minute. The three tiers are paced by throughputTimer at 12.5 / 25 / 12.5 RPM, so these lines should sit flat and well separated -- a slow tier drifting upward is the rpsThreadGroup overshoot bug documented in loadtest/README.md.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "count")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.statut == "all")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction != "all" and r.transaction != "internal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.transaction, set: ${transaction:json}))
  |> group(columns: ["transaction"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1m, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "transaction"])
  |> rename(columns: {transaction: "sampler"})

panels.title: Response time p95 by sampler
panels.description: The three latency tiers of wireglass-loadtest-stand should separate cleanly here: fast ~30ms, medium ~300ms, slow ~2s.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "pct95.0")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.statut == "all")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction != "all" and r.transaction != "internal")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.transaction, set: ${transaction:json}))
  |> group(columns: ["transaction"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "transaction"])
  |> rename(columns: {transaction: "sampler"})

panels.title: Response time percentiles (all samplers)
panels.description: avg / p90 / p95 / p99 across the whole plan, from the transaction=all rollup.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "avg" or r._field == "pct90.0" or r._field == "pct95.0" or r._field == "pct99.0")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction == "all" and r.statut == "all")
  |> group(columns: ["_field"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: Errors (all samplers)
panels.description: Flat zero is the expected state; any bar means the stand started rejecting or timing out. Not split by sampler on purpose: influxDbListener writes countError ONLY on the transaction=all rollup row, so a per-sampler version of this panel would read No data even during a real outage. To find which tier failed, compare the statut=ok and statut=all series in 'Throughput by sampler'.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "jmeter")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "jmeter" and r._field == "countError")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.transaction == "all" and r.statut == "all")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "errors")
